授業目的: This course examines contemporary Japanese society from multiple perspectives through various case studies. It encourages students to approach Japanese society in the global context by the application of general concepts of social analysis.
到達目標: After taking this course students will gain particular insights into contemporary Japanese society as well as relevant theoretical knowledge for analyzing socio-cultural issues comparatively.

教科書/Required texts0: Readings available in LUNA or free online.
学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student: Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy andcurriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.Asian Studies:1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.2. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.3. Intercultural understanding and multicultural coexistence: the ability to be sensitive to different cultures and to coexist with diverse cultures.
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
備考Note0: This course is designed to help non-native English speaking students to acquire analytical and presentation skills through the study of social issues in contemporary Japan. Students seeking advanced training in the study of Japanese society should consider other higher-level courses.

name: 【470】Contemporary Japanese Society and Culture／Contemporary Japanese Society and Culture [EN]
担当者: ツー　ティモシー（TSU TIMOTHY YUN HUI）
